payload.summary: Planned a coordinator-owned managed block in tasks.md that renders task status (checkboxes, claimed_by, completion timestamps) from coordinator-recorded issues. The renderer is invoked by pre-commit and post-merge hooks, falls back to a stale marker on coordinator failure, and is seeded by /plan-feature Gate 2 approval. No new coordinator schema; reuses existing managed-block helpers from plan-roadmap and HTTP helpers from coordination-bridge.

decisions.title: Reuse plan-roadmap managed-block markers
decisions.rationale: skills/plan-roadmap/scripts/renderer.py already implements _gen_block/_extract_human_sections with <!-- GENERATED: begin/end <name> --> markers. Adopting the existing convention (block name 'coordinator:tasks-status') avoids inventing a parallel pattern and inherits tested helpers.

decisions.title: Wire both pre-commit and post-merge hooks
decisions.rationale: Pre-commit catches drift the committer introduces; post-merge catches drift incoming from other agents' merged work. Coverage matrix in design.md (D2) established the two are non-overlapping. With Q3='stale marker' selected, neither hook can block git operations, removing the usual objection to network-dependent hooks.

decisions.title: Seed coordinator issues at /plan-feature Gate 2 approval
decisions.rationale: Earlier alternatives — seed at /implement-feature start, or lazily on first renderer invocation — were rejected. Gate-2 seeding means tasks.md has live coordinator state from the moment the plan is approved, with no dead window where the renderer would only emit stale markers.

decisions.title: Stale-marker fallback rather than silent skip or hard fail
decisions.rationale: User-selected at discovery Gate 0. Silent skip leaves last-known state without indication of staleness — defeats the trust-restoration goal. Hard fail couples local git operations to remote coordinator uptime. Stale marker surfaces the outage in PR diffs and repaints cleanly on recovery.

decisions.title: Skip Step 10 (resource-claim pre-registration)
decisions.rationale: Step 2 exploration confirmed neither in-flight change (add-engineering-methodology-skills, harness-engineering-features) competes for the files or logical keys this proposal claims. Pre-registering 12 zero-TTL planning claims would add HTTP and audit-log surface without surfacing new conflict information. Logged here so the skip is traceable.

decisions.title: Defer GET /issues/by-change/<id> convenience endpoint to a follow-up
decisions.rationale: list_issues(labels=['change:<id>']) suffices at OpenSpec scale (10-30 tasks per change). A dedicated endpoint would save client-side label filtering at the cost of added coordinator API surface. Revisit if the renderer becomes a hot path.

next_steps: Run /implement-feature add-coordinator-task-status-renderer once the user approves at Gate 2.
Start with wp-contracts (Phase 1) — the contracts/README.md is already drafted but should be reviewed against the spec scenarios.
wp-renderer-skill, wp-plan-feature-integration, wp-hooks can run in parallel after wp-contracts (parallel_zones validation confirmed 3 parallel pairs).

relevant_files.description: Approved proposal with selected Approach 1 and discovery decisions baked in
Architecture sketch, 7 decisions (D1-D7), and open questions
ADDED Requirements with 16 spec scenarios covering renderer, fallback, hooks, seeding, and packaging
29 tasks across 5 phases with TDD ordering and checkpoint markers
CLI contracts for renderer + seeder; managed-block format reference
5 packages, DAG: wp-contracts → {renderer, plan-feature, hooks} (parallel) → wp-integration
